items: put off: make sb not want to do or not like sth
get on for: be almost a particular time, number, age, etc
go off: be no longer fresh
run out of: not have any left
go on: continue happening or doing sth; do sth after doing sth else
take to: begin to like; begin to do sth regularly
go/come round: go/come to sb's house to visit them
turn out: develop in a particular way or have a particular result
turn up: appear unexpectedly or without making a firm arrangement
cook: a good/great/etc cook; cook a meal/chicken/etc; do the cooking
drink: make (sb) a drink; have a drink (of sth); drink sth; drink to sb; drink to sb's health; drink a toast to sb
feed: feed an animal/etc; feed on sth
fill: fill sth (up); filled with sth; full of sth
food: make/prepare/cook/serve food; fast/junk food; pet food; health food
meal: make/cook/have a meal; go out for a meal
note: make/take/keep (a) note of sth; note sth (down)
occasion: on this/that occasion; on occasion; on the occasion of sth; special occasion
recipe: follow a recipe; recipe book; recipe for disaster
table: lay/set/clear the table; book/reserve a table
wash: wash the dishes; wash one's hands; do the washing-up; dishwasher; washing machine
remember to do; remember sth/sb/doing; remember that
wait for sth/sb; wait (for sth) to do; wait and see
lack sth; lack of sth; lacking in sth
offer sb sth; offer sth (to sb); offer to do
grow → growth, grown-up, growing, grown, home-grown, grower
safe → unsafe, (un)safely, save, safety, saviour, saver

sections.title: A: Cooking verbs (pictures)
sections.instructions: Write a verb from the box under each picture. (fry, chop, bake, grate, whisk, slice, mix, roast, grill, stir, boil)

items.sentence: Picture 1
Picture 2

I particularly like Mexican and Indian kitchen / cuisine.
Frozen / Freezing fish is just as tasty as fresh fish.
My mum's the best cooker / cook in the world!
Once the kettle / teapot has boiled, pour the boiling water over the jelly cubes.
Excuse me. Could we have the catalogue / menu, please?
Boil the eggs for three minutes in a saucepan on the grill / hob / oven.
Any meat that's kept in the fridge / freezer should be defrosted thoroughly before cooking.
Grandma's having her new chef / cooker delivered next week.
Chilli con carne is one of my favourite plates / bowls / saucers / dishes.
Are you going to get your suit dry-cleaned for the Carlton's dinner / lunch party tomorrow night?
My friend Sally's a true vegetable / vegetarian / vegan, so she doesn't eat meat, fish or even any milk products like cheese!
Let's get a Chinese takeaway / fast food tonight.
Fizzy / Soft drinks are gassy because they've got carbon dioxide in them.

sections.title: C: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I've taken (1) ...... not booking a table in my real name for these restaurant reviews...
Earlier in the evening, some friends had come (2) ...... for a chat and mentioned La Clara.
We decided just to drop (3) ...... to see if there was a free table.
There wasn't - always a risk if you (4) ...... up without booking, of course - but we decided to wait.
Having arrived at about 9.30, it was getting (5) ...... for eleven before we were finally seated.
while we were having a drink at the bar, we ran (6) ...... the food critic of a rival newspaper.
unfortunately our meal turned (7) ...... to be far from wonderful.
The sauce tasted like the cream had (8) ...... off.
Fifteen minutes after my mushrooms had arrived, the waiter came over to say that they had (9) ...... out of salmon.
He went (10) ...... to say that they would be closing at midnight.
The head chef of La Clara likes to try (11) ...... one new dish every day.
the waiter kept (12) ...... coming over to see if we had finished.
I'll leave (13) ...... a description of the dessert.
I don't want to (14) ...... anyone off trying La Clara...

sections.title: D: Matching (Where do these texts appear?)
sections.instructions: Read ten different ways of explaining things and decide where these texts appear.

items.sentence: Only heat-resistant ceramic and glass dishes should be used for oven baking/roasting.
items.answer: C in the instructions for an electric cooker

items.sentence: A homemade chunky and succulent beefburger, filled with mozzarella cheese and served with a side salad and baked potato.
Wash the carrots and slice. Set to one side. Boil the potatoes for six minutes or until slightly soft to the touch.
Open seven days a week. Ideal for wedding receptions, birthday parties and other special occasions.
items.answer: G in an advertisement for a restaurant

items.sentence: Large plates, dishes and bowls should be loaded into the bottom rack. Glasses should be placed upside down on the top rack. Cutlery should be placed in the cutlery container.
items.answer: A in the instructions for a dishwasher

items.sentence: Are you fed up with trying to lose weight and failing? Doreen Brown asks top nutritionists how we can lose that fat, and not put it back on again.
items.answer: I in an article on dieting

items.sentence: Make a note of all the things you have to do before your guests arrive... If you're planning to cook the meal yourself... make sure you choose a menu you know well.
items.answer: J in an article on organising a dinner party

items.sentence: Whites and delicate items should be washed separately.
items.answer: B in the instructions for a washing machine

items.sentence: I ask you all to raise your glasses so we can drink to my wonderful great-grandfather, ninety-seven years young today!
items.answer: H in a toast at a celebration

items.sentence: Vegetarian and Vegan Products.
items.answer: E on a sign in a health food shop

sections.title: E: Word patterns (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Everyone complimented her ...... the wonderful buffet she'd laid on.
The problem with drinks like that is they're full ...... sugar.
I can't choose ...... Death by Chocolate or fruit salad.
I'm not going to tell you what's for supper. You'll just have to wait ...... see.
She's generally regarded ...... being the best cookery book writer of her generation.
Most people associate English food ...... fish and chips and shepherd's pie.
Karen's very careful ...... how much salt she has.
There's a lack ...... good restaurants round here.
The meat was well cooked, but the sauce was totally lacking ...... flavour.

sections.title: F: Verb patterns
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: I tend not ...... (cook) very often during the week as I don't have time.
Do you remember ...... (go) to that wonderful little taverna on Skiathos last summer?
I must remember ...... (buy) some bread on the way home.
Why don't you offer ...... (help) with the washing-up?
I'd suggest ...... (get) a takeaway rather than cooking.
Would you be willing ...... (lend) me a hand with the pudding?

sections.title: G: Word formation (text)
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

items.sentence: Good cooking is always a strange (1) ...... of science and art and you certainly have to be fairly (2) ...... if you want to come up with your own recipes.
All cooking also demands a fair amount of (3) ...... and this is doubly true when you're producing (4) ...... dishes.
You'll be (5) ...... by how honest people can be when it comes to food.
I've had people tell me my latest dish is (6) ...... and (7) ...... inedible!
Just smile (8) ...... and thank them for their valuable opinion.
And never forget that when you're waiting (9) ...... to hear whether or not your 'masterpiece' is a success and they suddenly show their (10) ...... you'll realise it was all worth it.
My tips: home- (11) ...... vegetables are always the tastiest.
Every good cook needs a top- quality (12) ....... It's an essential piece of kitchen equipment, not a luxury.
Make sure you've got lots of good- quality food storage (13) .......
And, finally, remember that designing a new dish is not about (14) ....... It's about taking risks, and learning from your mistakes.
